```python
# test_is_valid_email.py
import pytest

from your_module import is_valid_email  # <-- update import to match your project


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "email",
    [
        # Happy paths (valid)
        "test@example.com",
        "user.name+tag@example.co.uk",
        "USER_123@example-domain.com",
        "a@b.co",                 # minimal valid with 1-char local and 2+ TLD chars
        "john.doe@sub.example.com",
        "name%tag@domain.io",
        "first_last@domain.travel",
        "12345@letters123.net",
        "a.b-c_d%+@example.co",  # allowed characters in local part
    ],
)
def test_is_valid_email_happy_path_valid_emails(email):
    assert is_valid_email(email) is True


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "email",
    [
        # Edge cases / tricky formats that should be rejected by this regex
        "",                         # empty string
        " ",                        # whitespace only
        "a@b",                      # missing TLD
        "a@b.c",                    # TLD too short (needs {2,})
        "a@b..com",                 # double dot in domain labels
        "a@.com",                   # domain starts with dot
        "a@com.",                   # domain ends with dot
        "@example.com",            # missing local part
        "example.com",             # missing '@'
        "a@@example.com",         # multiple '@'
        "a@-example.com",          # domain label starts with '-'
        "a@example-.com",          # domain label ends with '-'
        "a@example..com",         # consecutive dots in domain part
        "a@example,com",          # invalid separator comma
        "a@example#com",          # invalid char in domain part
        "a ex@example.com",       # space in local part
        "a@exa mple.com",         # space in domain part
        "a@例子.测试",              # non-ASCII (regex only allows A-Za-z in TLD)
        "a@例子.com",               # non-ASCII in domain
        "a@domain.cøm",           # non-ASCII in TLD
        "a@domain.toolongtld",   # still valid by regex; not an edge case—left out
    ],
)
def test_is_valid_email_edge_cases_invalid_emails(email):
    assert is_valid_email(email) is False


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "email",
    [
        # Error cases: wrong types. The function expects str.
        None,
        123,
        45.6,
        ["a@b.com"],
        {"email": "a@b.com"},
        ("a@b.com",),
    ],
)
def test_is_valid_email_error_wrong_types(email):
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        is_valid_email(email)
```
